files_analyzed.file: dist/el-samples-text/Transportation/Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
files_analyzed.source_document: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.docx

scope_periods.description: Last three fiscal years ended November 30, 2022 (FY22), 2023 (FY23) and 2024 (FY24) and the latest available year-to-date information
scope_periods.source: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json

sections.heading: Overview
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Meet with Target's officers and management in order to develop an understanding of operations, including its:
Basis of financial information;
Organization structure;
Historical growth by geography; and
Finance function, financial reporting framework and internal control environment.

sections.heading: Consistency of accounting policies
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Consider if the following accounting policies have been applied consistently within and across historical periods:
Revenue recognition;
Significant accounting estimates (e.g. inventory costing, allowance for doubtful accounts, etc.);
Capitalization policies;
Depreciation policies; and
Recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles, procedures or estimates and provisions.

sections.heading: Quality / sustainability of earnings
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Identify, and where possible quantify, potential earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization ("EBITDA") normalization items by considering:
The impact of the normalization adjustments identified by Management;
Large, unusual or non-recurring events, or transactions that may have distorted results;
The impact of any changes to senior management or management team structure;
The impact of provisions (allowance for doubtful accounts, inventory obsolescence, other), management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results;
The impact of foreign currency transactions;
The impact of related party transactions and shared services provided by other entities, if applicable;
The impact of any changes in accounting policies, procedures and estimates; and
Other potential items identified during the due diligence process.

sections.heading: Revenue and profitability trends
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's revenue and margins and inquire about:
Revenue and profitability by significant customer;
Revenue and profitability by customer type;
Revenue and profitability by customer profile (e.g., related party vs. third party);
Revenue and profitability by trip type (e.g., tours, day trips, etc.);
Impact of seasonality, if any;
Any non-recurring components of revenue; and
Cut off procedures.
For each of the above items a) – d), inquire about key performance indicators such as volume (e.g., number of trips, etc.).

sections.heading: Cost of revenues and Operating expenses
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of expenses and inquire about:
Direct costs (labour and benefits, etc.);
Employee compensation and related costs by function;
Selling, general, and administrative expenses;
Rent and utilities expense;
Historical operating expense trend as a % of revenue; and
Unusual and extraordinary items.

sections.heading: Working capital
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Comment on working capital trends for the past 24 months;
Adjust working capital for any significant items which are considered to be non-recurring in nature;
Comment on the seasonality of working capital;
Comment on cash management procedures; and
Understand trade ratio trends.

sections.heading: Accounts receivable
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of accounts receivable and inquire and comment on:
Trade and non-trade balances;
Aging analysis and turnover analysis; and
Allowance for uncollectible accounts, write-offs and reserves.

sections.heading: Inventory
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of inventory accounts and inquire and comment on:
Costing methodology;
Composition (e.g. raw materials, supplies, finished goods etc.); and
Turnover, aging and provisions.

sections.heading: Other assets and liabilities
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Prepare a summary of other assets and liabilities (including prepaid expenses and deposits, deferred revenue and customer deposits) and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances.

sections.heading: Accounts payable and accrued liabilities
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of accounts payable, accrued liabilities and customer deposits and inquire about:
Accounts payable aging and days outstanding;
Accrued liabilities;
Supplier settlement terms; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.

sections.heading: Capital expenditure and fixed assets
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of historic and future capital cost requirements including:
Maintenance versus growth capital expenditures;
Other historical, deferred, and planned capital expenditures;
Historical replacement cycle and costs;
Utilization per unit; and
Impairment write-downs and other issues.

sections.heading: Net Debt
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Prepare a summary of debt and debt-like items (e.g. notes payable, letters of credit, capital vendor payables, etc.), other assets, and liabilities and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances.

sections.heading: Related Party Transactions
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Inquire about and summarize related party transactions including:
Nature and extent of related party transactions; and
Basis of pricing for arrangements with related parties.

sections.heading: Commitments and Contingencies
appears_in: Project Bus - Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work  1 1.json
bullets: Inquire about significant commitments and contingent liabilities including:
Off-balance sheet liabilities;
Purchase commitments;
Operating and capital leases;
Incentive compensation and employee benefit obligations;
Expected or contingent liabilities (e.g. environmental, litigation, regulatory and tax); and
Pending or threatened litigation or investigations.

notes: This SOW (Project Bus) is for a transportation/coach company (Great Canadian Coaches Inc., Great Canadian Fleet Services Inc., Great Canadian Holidays Inc., Big Blue Coach Inc., and 966021 Ontario Ltd.).
The document is structured as a Financial Due Diligence Work Plan with no separate phases (single phase covering all FDD scope).

The document contains two sub-heading groupings: 'Supporting analyses to quality / sustainability of earnings' (covering Revenue and profitability trends, Cost of revenues and Operating expenses) and 'Supporting analyses to working capital' (covering Accounts receivable, Inventory, Other assets and liabilities, Accounts payable and accrued liabilities).

Industry-specific content is most visible in the Revenue section, which breaks down analysis by trip type (tours, day trips, etc.) and uses KPIs like number of trips — typical of a bus/coach transportation business.

Capital expenditure section includes fleet-specific items: maintenance versus growth capex, historical replacement cycle and costs, and utilization per unit — reflecting the fleet-intensive nature of the transportation industry.

Some standard transportation industry terms (fleet management, route-based operations, fuel costs, ridership, fare revenue, contract transit, regulatory compliance) do not appear verbatim in this document but are included in industry_specific_terms as standard transportation FDD scope items.
The fee structure distinguishes between an Excel workbook deliverable ($35,000-$60,000) and a written report (additional $20,000-$25,000), which is common for FDD engagements.
